ddls:
CREATE TABLE "api_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API request */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of HTTP request (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* API endpoint requested */,
  "payload" TEXT /* Payload sent with the request */,
  "response_code" INTEGER /* HTTP response code received */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response content received */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the request was made */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was received */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the request */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the request */,
  "request_duration" INTEGER /* Duration in milliseconds to process the request */,
  "error_details" TEXT /* Detailed error message if the request fails */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the user making the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_requests_endpoint FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint") REFERENCES api_endpoints ("endpoint"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES api_users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API endpoint */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* URL of the API endpoint */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the API endpoint */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method supported by the endpoint */,
  "rate_limit" INTEGER /* Rate limit for the endpoint (requests per minute) */,
  "security_level" TEXT /* Security level required for the endpoint (e.g., public, internal, admin) */,
  "sla_compliance" TEXT /* SLA compliance metrics (e.g., 99.9% uptime) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key for authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "api_key_expiration" TEXT /* Expiration date of the API key */,
  "token" TEXT /* JWT or other token for authentication */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
